Blood Test Results — Comprehensive Metabolic Panel: Complete blood count shows hemoglobin 13.8 g/dL, WBC 7,200/µL, and platelets 224,000/µL, all within normal limits. The comprehensive metabolic panel reveals mildly elevated ALT at 52 U/L (reference 7–40), suggesting possible hepatic steatosis. Fasting glucose is 118 mg/dL (borderline, reference 70–99), and HbA1c is 6.1%, consistent with prediabetes. Lipid panel shows LDL cholesterol of 142 mg/dL; physician has recommended dietary intervention and repeat testing in three months.
